channel.title: Cool Event (2026 and Beyond) Posts on My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content in Cool Event (2026 and Beyond) Posts on My New Hugo Site

item.title: AI4Healthcare Workshop (Jan. 2026)
item.description: This event I went to at the beginning of the year in January (Sat. 1/10/26). I decided to book a plane ticket on a whim to the San Francisco Bay Area and attend a healthcare workshop/hackathon.This was not typically something I would do, but I did it for three reasons: 1) I wanted to experience and see for myself what the tech/startup scene was like in the Bay, and 2) I wanted to do some networking for career purposes. I’ve worked hard and built a strong network on the East Coast (think D.C., Baltimore, Philadelphia, and to an extent New York City), but the West Coast was totally unknown to me. I felt like this was an opportunity too good to pass up because of who the sponsor was: Mithril.ai, which is a biotech startup that recently raised a couple million dollars. Moreover, I could not pass on this opportunity because of where the event was located: AGI House, a hacker house that I’m intimately familiar with; I was at both hackathons they hosted in D.C. in 2025. 3) Lastly, I could not pass up the event because two of the three datasets that they provided were NIH datasets, and I have a small healthcare background having worked before for a healthcare startup and as an NIH intern when I was 19. I had never been to the Bay Area except for the airport once and did not know what to expect. As someone who doesn’t travel much and doesn’t really enjoy traveling at all, I was somewhat nervous to go on the trip. I hesitated, and then decided fck it* and booked the plane ticket thinking, why not? I set an aggressive timeline: I booked a United flight on Tue. 1/6/26, I would fly out on the evening of Fri. 1/9/26 (which incidentally was my birthday lol), stay the night at an Airbnb, attend the event the next day Sat. 1/10/26, take a red-eye flight back to the East Coast as soon as the event ended, and arrive in Baltimore the morning of Sun. 1/11/26.
